results.title: Quipu — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Wikipedia's Quipu page directly covers the source domain mechanism: knot types, ply, color, decimal position, and pendant/subsidiary hierarchy—all the source-side content words.

results.title: Khipu Field Guide — Signs and Schematics
results.judge_reason: A khipu sign-system schematic specifically describing positional decimal encoding, pendant clusters, and top-cord sums maps precisely onto the candidate's source mechanism including the verification anchor.

results.title: The Inka khipu — Smarthistory
results.judge_reason: Cultural-context overview that explicitly mentions top-cord sums verifying pendant sums (the consistency-check mechanism that is the candidate's core distinct feature).

results.title: Compressed Chain of Thought (CCoT, 2024/12)
results.judge_reason: CCoT compresses CoT to dense latent representations but lacks the multi-axis structured-summary-with-sum-consistency anchor that defines the candidate's novel mechanism.

results.title: Chain Of Thought Compression: Theoretical Analysis (2026/1)
results.judge_reason: Theoretical analysis of CoT compression learning-signal decay; does not propose the structured-summary cord encoding or sum-consistency verification.

results.title: Verifying CoT via Computational Graph (2025/10)
results.judge_reason: Attribution-graph verification of CoT touches on verification but uses causal-information-flow approximation rather than hierarchical sum-consistency at a summary anchor.

results.title: Reinforcing Structured CoT for Video (2026/3)
results.judge_reason: Video-focused SDRL using key visual cues for anchoring; the 'anchoring' concept is loosely related but neither structured-multi-axis nor sum-consistency-verified.

results.title: Enhancing CoT with Iterative Summarization (2025/1)
results.judge_reason: Iterative summarization is the closest LLM-side neighbor to summary-anchor framing, but it lacks the multi-axis encoding and the explicit local sum-consistency verification mechanism.
